{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query asks for the most effective, scientifically proven, and cost-efficient caries prevention program in children. The documents provided discuss various caries prevention methods, including pit and fissure sealants, which are mentioned as being highly effective in reducing caries risk. Document [11] states that one study demonstrated a 37% reduction in caries risk with pit and fissure sealants compared to a control group. Additionally, Document [11] mentions that over a 3-year period, first permanent molars treated with sealants showed a 44% lower risk of developing caries compared to untreated teeth. While oral health education (Document [9]) and ART (Document [13]) are also discussed, the evidence for pit and fissure sealants is more robust and specific. Enameloplasty is not mentioned in the documents.", "answer_choice": "C"}
